evidence_quotes.quote: They have sold games in unfinished state.
They use gambling tricks like lootboxes
nickel and dime people by selling a product in piece.
They have the ability to just take our games from us after we bought them.

paragraphs.text: For too long gaming companies avoided the law by simple virtue of not being noticed. They have sold games in unfinished state.(Dawn of war 3, Pathfinder wrath of the rightious, Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition, Cyberpunk 2077) Broke them with update. Or sold one thing then changed it compleetly(Stellaris) They use gambling tricks like lootboxes(Which i believe you guys fixed) trying to nickel and dime people by selling a product in piece. But worst of all: They have the ability to just take our games from us after we bought them. This is the kind of crap gamers had to put up with for decades because we coudnt trust the goverments to accauly punish companies that pull this kind of stuff. The eu gives me hope that meaby. Just meaby. We finaly have a goverment body willing to stand up for us. The digital fairness act should include stuff that will help protect our hobbies from greedy corperations that have been actively screwing up over. And while your add it could you please do something about mastercard and visa blackmailing gamestores into not selling stuff. Even if the stuff they target is distastefull that should be the goverments job not a corperation to desided.
